name: Post-push Product Frontend Backlog Selection Gate
source_gate: Phase 21.2 explicit local commit and push approval gate
decision: After the Phase 21 local delivery commit/push completed, select product/frontend backlog as the next branch. Do not enter Pi delivery planning, Go file-read parity assessment, or Go ownership expansion from this gate.

post_push_truth.live_pi_verification_reason: 21.3 selects product/frontend backlog and does not authorize Pi deploy, service reload, Caddy reload, or live runtime mutation.

repo_local_omx_removal.runtime_truth: The repo-local .omx/ directory was removed once after path verification, but active Codex/OMX native hooks may recreate ignored .omx/ state during subsequent tool calls. Durable removal requires disabling or uninstalling the global hook separately.

not_selected.reason: Would require a separate live preflight, rollback plan, privacy/artifact sweep, and deployment authorization.
Text attachment body search remains Python-owned and needs a separate file-read safety/data-dir/path traversal/performance/rollback contract before assessment or implementation.
Phase 20 closed without expanding Go beyond the hardened read-only DB surface.

source_docs: Prism_Go_模組逐步重構計劃報告.md
product_frontend_intake.selection_rule: Start with a read-only product/frontend backlog audit, then promote exactly one smallest workflow-safe item. Prefer existing React/Vite/Zustand/Tailwind patterns and existing API contracts.
allowed_assessment_scope: Current frontend route and workflow inventory
Reading/editor workflow gaps that preserve existing Preview Editing UX
Prompt Builder and Settings layout gaps that only reorganize existing features
not_allowed_by_21_3: New backend API or schema implementation
AI/ML, collaboration, realtime, plugin platform, or collections schema
Pi deploy or live service reload
Go file-read/body scan or Go write/file/migration implementation

verification_required_for_21_3: tests/test_phase21_post_push_product_frontend_selection.py locks the branch selection and forbidden runtime scope
tests/test_phase21_local_commit_push_readiness.py continues to lock local delivery boundaries

allowed_next_step.title: Product Frontend Backlog Intake Gate
allowed_next_step.scope: Plan-only read-only audit of product/frontend backlog candidates, followed by selection of one smallest workflow-safe frontend/product item. This does not authorize implementation until the selected item is explicitly approved.
not_authorized_without_approval: New backend API or DB schema
Pi deploy or live service reload
